analysis:
1. US-Iran Military Escalation

The Iran conflict cluster dominates by volume and shows meaningful divergence across timeframes. "US forces enter Iran by December 31" sits at 70% but has collapsed -13.5pp this week — the single largest directional move in this cluster. "US forces enter Iran by March 31" holds at 42% with only -1pp weekly drift, while "Will the U.S. invade Iran by March 31" sits at 20.1%, down -3pp on the week. The ceasefire ladder prices resolution at 16% by March 31, 36% by April 30, 53% by May 31, and 58% by June 30 — a shallow curve suggesting the market expects prolonged conflict rather than quick resolution. "Iran agrees to end enrichment by March 31" nudged up +2pp this week to just 4%, indicating near-zero expectation of a diplomatic breakthrough in the near term.

2. Trump-China Diplomacy Surge

"Will Trump visit China by March 31" is the most explosive single market this week, jumping +67pp to 66.8% — a near-zero-to-majority move in one week, with virtually the entire month-change accounted for this week. The April 30 version prices at 87%, up +3pp weekly and +14pp monthly, confirming the market views a Trump China visit as highly probable within weeks. This is new information being rapidly priced in; the March 31 market's -5.4pp day-change suggests some pullback on the tightest deadline after the initial surge. Combined volume across both markets is ~$2.3M, signaling genuine conviction.

3. Venezuela Regime & Opposition Collapse

The Venezuela leadership cluster shows near-universal rejection of US-installed figures: Grenell at 0.1%, Donovan at 0.2%, Hegseth at 0.2%, Caine at 0.1%. Delcy Rodríguez leads at 60% to remain in power end-2026, up +8pp over the month. María Corina Machado's return markets are in freefall — the January 31 deadline market sits at 0.2% (down -3.3pp this week), and the March 31 version at 11%, down -3.5pp this week and -18.5pp over the month. Maduro release from custody prices at effectively zero across both deadline markets. The market has decisively concluded the Maduro-aligned government survives and the opposition gains no foothold.

4. Greenland & Mexico Territorial Ambitions Fade

"Will Trump acquire Greenland before 2027" — the highest-volume market at $29.9M — sits at just 10.2% with virtually no weekly movement (-0.15pp). "US acquire part of Greenland in 2026" is at 16%, down -2pp this week and -10pp over the month, while a Trump-Denmark deal by March 31 prices at just 1.5%. The invasion scenario prices at 6%. Mexico military action is similarly deflating: the anti-cartel ground operation by March 31 dropped -8.5pp this week to 9%, and a US strike on Mexico by January 31 fell -0.8pp to 0.4%. Both territorial ambition themes are losing market conviction in parallel.

5. Key Shifts This Week

**Trump China visit (+67pp):** "Will Trump visit China by March 31" went from effectively zero to 66.8% in a single week — the largest absolute weekly move in the dataset by far, on $1.4M volume. **US forces enter Iran by December 31 (-13.5pp):** Dropped from ~83.5% to 70%, the sharpest bearish reversal in the Iran cluster, suggesting reduced near-term escalation expectations. **Epstein client list (-11.75pp):** Now priced at 100% (already resolved/settled), but the -11.75pp weekly reading reflects end-of-market price normalization. **Anti-cartel Mexico operation (-8.5pp):** Fell to 9% by March 31, markets pricing out imminent ground action. **"Trump out as President by March 31" (-0.85pp to 1.3%):** Marginal but consistent downward drift; the longer-dated "before 2027" version holds at 16%, down -1pp weekly.
